name: Basic Buttercream Frosting
recipeIngredient: 3 large egg whites, at room temperature
1 1/3 cups (2 sticks plus 5 tablespoons) unsalted butter, softened
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large, grease-free bowl, combine the egg whites and sugar. Set the bowl over a pot of hot, not simmering, water (the water should touch the bottom of the bowl). Whisk the mixture constantly until it is white and creamy and reaches 110-186°F. Test by rubbing a small amount between your thumb and forefinger to ensure all sugar crystals are dissolved. Remove from hot water.
2. Using a hand-held electric mixer on medium speed, beat the egg white mixture for 5-6 minutes until cool and forms a thick, shiny meringue.
3. In a second large bowl, beat butter on medium speed until creamy. Gradually add the meringue, beating in vanilla extract. Continue mixing until the buttercream is thick and smooth. (For chocolate variation, beat 9 ounces of melted, tepid semisweet or bittersweet chocolate into the buttercream with vanilla.)
